config: Parent directory of Path must exist before StoreSession is called (created by client.go:Run)

risks: AcceptTermsOfService silently accepts without presenting ToS text to the user or confirming consent
prompt() uses fmt.Print (not a logger), mixing authentication interaction with any other stdout output in the process
API() returns nil if called before or after Run() — callers that store the *tg.Client reference and use it outside the Run callback will panic on method calls
debug bool causes verbose raw message dumps to os.Stdout (including %#v of full gotd structs). If true in production, this leaks message content and floods logs with unstructured output.
No file locking; concurrent processes sharing the same session path can produce a torn write or read a partially written session
patterns: AuthFlow interface is a thin re-export of auth.UserAuthenticator — it adds no new surface but decouples client.go from the gotd auth package type directly
